expectations.text: Dockerfile uses multi-stage build with a separate builder and runtime stage
expectations.evidence: Uses 'FROM golang:1.23-bookworm AS builder' and 'FROM gcr.io/distroless/static AS runner' — two distinct stages.

expectations.text: CGO_ENABLED=0 is set in the build command to produce a static binary
expectations.evidence: 'CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -o /app/server ./cmd/server' explicitly sets CGO_ENABLED=0.

expectations.text: Runtime stage uses gcr.io/distroless/static or scratch (not a full OS image)
expectations.evidence: Runtime stage is 'FROM gcr.io/distroless/static AS runner'.

expectations.text: USER directive sets a non-root user (UID 65534 or similar) in the runtime stage
expectations.evidence: 'USER nonroot' — distroless provides UID 65532 as the nonroot user.

expectations.text: ENTRYPOINT uses exec form (JSON array), not shell form
expectations.evidence: 'ENTRYPOINT ["/server"]' uses exec/JSON array form.

expectations.text: BuildKit cache mounts are used for Go module cache (/go/pkg/mod)
expectations.evidence: '--mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod' is used in both the download and build steps.

expectations.text: Dockerfile uses multi-stage build with a separate builder and runtime stage
expectations.evidence: Uses 'FROM golang:1.22-alpine AS builder' and 'FROM scratch' — two distinct stages.

expectations.text: CGO_ENABLED=0 is set in the build command to produce a static binary
expectations.evidence: 'RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build ...' explicitly sets CGO_ENABLED=0.

expectations.text: Runtime stage uses gcr.io/distroless/static or scratch (not a full OS image)
expectations.evidence: Runtime stage is 'FROM scratch' — minimal base with zero attack surface.

expectations.text: USER directive sets a non-root user (UID 65534 or similar) in the runtime stage
expectations.evidence: 'USER 65534:65534' sets the nobody UID in the runtime stage.

expectations.text: ENTRYPOINT uses exec form (JSON array), not shell form
expectations.evidence: 'ENTRYPOINT ["/server"]' uses exec/JSON array form.

expectations.text: BuildKit cache mounts are used for Go module cache (/go/pkg/mod)
expectations.evidence: Uses plain 'RUN go mod download' without --mount=type=cache. No BuildKit cache mounts present.

expectations.text: Recommends pinning the image tag away from 'latest' to a specific version
expectations.evidence: Changes to 'lscr.io/linuxserver/qbittorrent:4.6.7' with comment '# pin version, never :latest'.

expectations.text: Recommends adding security_opt with no-new-privileges=true
expectations.evidence: Adds 'security_opt: - no-new-privileges=true' with explanation about setuid/setgid escalation.

expectations.text: Explains that PUID/PGID images (LinuxServer.io) still start as root before dropping privileges
expectations.evidence: 'LinuxServer images start the entrypoint as root before dropping to PUID/PGID. This is less secure than images that never run as root at all.'

expectations.text: Provides a concrete updated compose.yaml snippet or diff showing the improvements
expectations.evidence: Provides a complete updated compose.yaml with all security changes applied and annotated.

expectations.text: Recommends pinning the image tag away from 'latest' to a specific version
expectations.evidence: The compose snippet still shows 'image: lscr.io/linuxserver/qbittorrent:latest' — the tag is not pinned.

expectations.text: Recommends adding security_opt with no-new-privileges=true
expectations.evidence: Adds 'security_opt: - no-new-privileges:true' in the compose snippet.

expectations.text: Explains that PUID/PGID images (LinuxServer.io) still start as root before dropping privileges
expectations.evidence: Point 3 explains 'no-new-privileges: Prevents privilege escalation inside the container' and the cap_drop/cap_add discussion acknowledges the root start.

expectations.text: Provides a concrete updated compose.yaml snippet or diff showing the improvements
expectations.evidence: Provides a full updated compose.yaml snippet with security_opt, cap_drop, read_only, and tmpfs applied.

expectations.text: Compose file defines a named volume with driver: local and driver_opts for NFS (type: nfs, addr pointing to 192.168.1.50, device with the share path)
expectations.evidence: nfs_data volume uses 'driver: local' with driver_opts type: nfs, device: ':/volume1/nextcloud'.

expectations.text: The NFS volume uses driver_opts with 'o' field containing addr=192.168.1.50
expectations.evidence: 'o: "addr=192.168.1.50,nolock,hard,timeo=600,retrans=3,nfsvers=4"' is present in driver_opts.

expectations.text: Compose file includes security hardening (user directive, security_opt no-new-privileges, or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Both services include 'security_opt: - no-new-privileges=true' and 'cap_drop: - ALL'.

expectations.text: Services use depends_on or healthcheck for startup ordering if a database is included
expectations.evidence: db service has a healthcheck, and nextcloud uses 'depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy'.

expectations.text: Compose file defines a named volume with driver: local and driver_opts for NFS (type: nfs, addr pointing to 192.168.1.50, device with the share path)
expectations.evidence: Volume defines driver_opts but omits the explicit 'driver: local' field — the expectation requires both driver: local and driver_opts to be present.

expectations.text: The NFS volume uses driver_opts with 'o' field containing addr=192.168.1.50
expectations.evidence: 'o: addr=192.168.1.50,rw,nolock,soft' is present in driver_opts.

expectations.text: Compose file includes security hardening (user directive, security_opt no-new-privileges, or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Both services include 'security_opt: - no-new-privileges:true' and 'cap_drop: - ALL'.

expectations.text: Services use depends_on or healthcheck for startup ordering if a database is included
expectations.evidence: db has a healthcheck and nextcloud uses 'depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy' for startup ordering.
